name: Malignant lymphoma
description: Malignant lymphoma — malignancy of the lymphatic system (Hodgkin and non-Hodgkin); one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.

en.name: Malignant lymphoma
en.shortDescription: Malignant lymphoma — malignancy of the lymphatic system (Hodgkin and non-Hodgkin); one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Malignant lymphoma is a malignancy of the lymphatic system, comprising Hodgkin lymphoma and the many non-Hodgkin lymphomas. In intensive care it is a comorbidity associated with immunosuppression and chemotherapy complications, and a cause of admission. Lymphoma is one of the chronic health items in the APACHE IV severity model.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *432571 / 118600007 — Malignant lymphoma* with all descendants included, so it captures Hodgkin and the full range of non-Hodgkin lymphoma subtypes.

## Mapping Notes

- Leukemia and multiple myeloma are distinct haematologic malignancies captured by their own sets.
